commit.message:
feat(model-3d): add arc face splitting with edge snapping

- Add splitFaceByCurve kernel function that splits a face along a
  multi-segment curve path. First splits with a straight edge between
  boundary endpoints, then iteratively inserts intermediate vertices
  along the arc path via splitEdge + vertex repositioning.
- Add findPointOnBoundary helper to locate which face edge a point
  lies on, with tolerance for floating-point errors.
- Add edge snapping to ArcTool using existing snap infrastructure
  (generateAllSnapCandidates, snapPoint) for both start and end points.
- Replace D-shape ExtrudeGeometry with TubeGeometry for standalone
  arc creation — arcs are open curves, not enclosed shapes.
- Add targetFaceIndex to ArcTool DrawingState for HEMesh face lookup.
- Add 4 tests for splitFaceByCurve covering straight curves, multi-point
  arcs, boundary validation, and coplanarity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Telegram "no response generated" — synthesis, person fields, calendar range (#44)

Three root causes for empty/wrong Telegram responses:

1. When the LLM exhausted all 5 tool rounds, the synthesis round ran
   without tools AND without any instruction to produce text. The LLM
   saw a conversation ending in tool results and produced nothing.
   Fix: add explicit synthesis instruction + fallback from tool results.

2. person_info omitted birthday, company, and position fields — data
   from Apple Contacts was fetched but never shown to the LLM.

3. Calendar search hardcoded ±30 days, missing recurring events like
   birthdays months away. Widened default to ±180 days.

Also: gitignore live e2e test file (contains real names for integration
tests against live server).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve 31 security, quality, and debugging issues

Security (must-fix):
- Add -- end-of-options before prompt args in AI subprocess calls
- Remove diagnostic payload dump (last-stop-payload.json)
- Validate transcript_path stays within ~/.claude/ (path traversal)
- Validate plan path stays within project dir (path traversal)
- Fix defaults.json skip_patterns: true → [] (type mismatch)
- Fix git push diff ref to use upstream tracking branch
- Fix review text duplicated N× per question
- Add stderr warnings on fail-open errors

Quality (should-fix):
- Session-specific pending-review filename using parent PID
- Clear environment before spawning AI subprocesses (PATH+HOME only)
- Fail on missing HOME instead of /tmp fallback
- Extract duplicated truncate() to shared utils module (4→1 copy)
- Use LazyLock for static regex compilation
- Check git diff exit status
- Case-insensitive plan.md regex matching
- Replace sh -c with direct Command::new("git")
- Use &Path instead of &PathBuf in parallel_review
- Capture AI provider stderr instead of /dev/null

Nice-to-have:
- 10MB stdin size limit
- Unknown gate warning
- AiOutcome::Failed now carries reason string
- has_critical() avoids double format()+to_uppercase() allocation
- ResponseGate proper Default impl
- HookOutput builder pattern
- is_api_error hardened against false positives (starts_with check)
- Gate script avoids slow brew --prefix call
- truncate uses char count instead of byte indexing
- Unit tests for extract_json_response, is_api_error, has_critical

commit.message:
fix Caps Lock detection for Bluetooth keyboards

Bluetooth keyboards send KeyCode(vk=255) as RELEASE-only events instead
of Key.caps_lock PRESS events. Handle both key codes and both event types.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix map change override persistence via scene name tracking

Track map name via SceneManager.GetActiveScene() to reliably detect map
changes across all lifecycle hooks. On same-map restarts, skip re-apply
(prefabs stay modified). On map change, clear vanilla cache and re-apply
with OMRevertAll for clean OM state. Triple safety net: Patch_GameInit,
Patch_GameRestart, and OnGameStartedLogic all detect map changes.

commit.message:
Always re-encode audio to AAC-LC for Instagram/TikTok/FB downloads

postprocessor-args only runs during the merge step — if yt-dlp selects
a single combined stream (no merge), HE-AAC audio passes through unchanged
and many players/WhatsApp cannot decode it. Added an explicit ffmpeg
post-download step (ensure_aac) that always re-encodes the audio to
standard AAC-LC 128k/44100Hz stereo for Instagram/TikTok/FB mp4 files.

reliably.
